_README: INTERFACE MATRIX — FILL-IN TEMPLATE
PURPOSE
Capture every interface specification (Step 08 + Step 09 of the KB).
Feed this JSON to `interface_matrix_from_json.py` to produce a multi-tab
Excel workbook with one tab per subsystem.
WORKFLOW (RECOMMENDED — ONE SUBSYSTEM AT A TIME)
1. Copy this file:  cp interface-matrix-template.json my-matrix.json
2. Fill in the `subsystems` list (every subsystem in your system).
3. Fill in ONE subsystem's tab in `tabs` and run:
python3 interface_matrix_from_json.py my-matrix.json --only <ss_id>
Open the generated `my-matrix__<ss_id>.xlsx` and verify the layout.
4. Repeat for the next subsystem until every tab is filled.
5. Generate the combined workbook:
python3 interface_matrix_from_json.py my-matrix.json
ROW MODEL
Each row in a tab is one of:
GROUP HEADER  — set `group` only. Text goes in the Q column.
SPEC ROW      — set `name` only. Text goes in the R column (sub-row).
GROUP+VALUE   — set BOTH `group` AND value/units/provided_to. Text in
Q; the same row carries the value (used when the group
itself is the spec, e.g. "Total Cost Allowed = $1500").
Every row may carry `value`, `units`, `estimate`, `provided_to` (list of
subsystem IDs), `last_updated`, `last_updated_by`, `champion`, `est_due`,
`actual_due`. Omit fields that don't apply.
`value` may be a number, a string ("Bearer JWT", "See OpenAPI v1.2"),
or an Excel formula prefixed with `=` (e.g. `=E14*4/1000`).
RULES (from `08 - Creating the Interface Matrix.md` / `09 - Adding Values...`)
- One tab per subsystem. The owning subsystem's column is left blank
(you don't 'Provide To' yourself).
- Mark every other subsystem that consumes a spec with `Provided To`.
- Always separate value from units (E and F columns).
- Mark estimates with `"estimate": true` (renders as `X` in column G).
- Set `final_value_due` (Actual Due Date) even for unknown values, so the
timeline is visible to all teams.
- Interface names should match your DFD / N² Chart / Sequence Diagram
labels for traceability.

project.name: Interface Matrix
project._starting_row_number: First value of the Row # column (P4). Increments via Excel formula.

subsystems._instructions: Order matters — defines the left-to-right column order on every tab.
subsystems.name: Subsystem One

tabs._instructions: One key per subsystem id. Each value is a list of rows for that subsystem's tab.

ss1._comment: Pure group header — only Q column gets text.
Spec row under the group above — R column gets text, plus value/provided_to.
ss1.name: Detail Spec A

ss1._comment: Group with its own value (no detail rows underneath).
